message:
refactor: improve idiomatic Rust patterns and expand test coverage across engine modules

Apply idiomatic Rust improvements and expand tests across several modules:

- hindsight_trace_escalator: use let-chains instead of nested if-let,
  struct literal initialization with ..Default::default(), remove
  unnecessary & borrows. Expand integration tests from ~79 to ~490 lines
  with additional coverage for escalation policies, cooldown behavior,
  and capacity management.

- bounded_feedback_controller: derive Default for ControllerState
  instead of manual impl (all fields are zero/false), remove unnecessary
  & borrows, use let-chains and struct-init patterns.

- tier_eligibility_substrate: parameterize is_feedback_stable() with an
  explicit min_probe_samples argument instead of hardcoding 10, allowing
  policies to control the minimum sample threshold. Add tests for
  policy-driven probe sample sensitivity.

- third_party_verifier: include verifier name in attestation summary
  output ("verifier=name@version" instead of "verifier=version") so
  operators can identify which verifier produced an attestation.

- module_resolver, react_module_graph_verifier: apply let-chain
  refactoring for idiomatic pattern matching.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
